governed_surface_delta_AWAITING_HITL_SIG.status: PROPOSED — NOT SIGNED. The string 'HITL-SIGNED' was NOT written anywhere. The `_comment` change log says '***0.8.20 Slice 5d (R-20-E4, erasure) — AWAITING HITL SIGN-OFF, NOT SIGNED.***' verbatim.
governed_surface_delta_AWAITING_HITL_SIG.why_the_edit_was_made_at_all: The allowlist is a POSITIVE allowlist (live surface must be a SUBSET of it), so shipping the verb without allowlisting it turns the branch red in three suites. The edit is the minimum needed to keep the branch green and is clearly marked as a proposal.

additions_to_rust_GOVERNED_SURFACE_ALLOW.SourceId: 5c's provenance newtype — carried per 5c follow-up FU-5c-2. MUST be public: the facade re-exports PreparedWrite and Engine::write is public, so without the constructor a facade consumer cannot perform a canonical write at all.
additions_to_rust_GOVERNED_SURFACE_ALLOW.ExciseReport: erase_source's return type; MOVED from the operator-gated re-export block to the always-present one

non_command_types_added.rust_operator_only: OrphanProvenanceReport, OrphanProvenanceSource (CLI-only, no SDK parity — same posture as dump-mutations)

governed_surface_delta_AWAITING_HITL_SIG.operator_seam_type_count: unchanged at 20 — ExciseReport moved OUT as OrphanProvenanceReport/Source moved IN
governed_surface_delta_AWAITING_HITL_SIG.deliberately_NOT_allowlisted: `excise_source` remains CLI-only. It stays the recovery seam and is the ONLY verb permitted to address the engine's reserved `_`-prefixed provenance namespace.

item_9b_erase_source.shape: Engine::excise_source was split into a shared private `erase_source_shared(verb, source_id)` with TWO public spellings over ONE engine path, per the design's 'one engine path with excise_source'. `excise_source_inner` was un-gated (still private) so both spellings share it; nothing is duplicated.
item_9b_erase_source.erase_source: UNGATED (governed SDK). Available on the default, operator-OFF facade.

JUDGEMENT_CALL_FOR_REVIEW — asymmetric v.what: `erase_source` validates via `SourceId::new` (construct-to-validate) and therefore REJECTS the reserved `_`-prefixed namespace; `excise_source` keeps its old permissive `is_empty()` check.
JUDGEMENT_CALL_FOR_REVIEW — asymmetric v.why_it_is_not_an_oversight: R-20-E8 REQUIRES an operator to be able to run `excise_source('_legacy:pre-0.8.20')`, and 5c pins exactly that at provenance_mandatory.rs:162. Making the two validate identically would have broken that pin. Conversely, letting the GOVERNED verb reach `_legacy:pre-0.8.20` would let one SDK call erase every pre-0.8.20 anonymous row at once. The asymmetry is the safety boundary, and it is documented at the call site, in the docs, and in the commit message.
JUDGEMENT_CALL_FOR_REVIEW — asymmetric v.reviewer_should_confirm: that this asymmetry is intended and correctly located

item_9b_erase_source.drain_constant: erase_source_shared uses LIFECYCLE_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS (ungated, 30s) rather than REBUILD_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS (operator-gated, also 30s). Same budget; the swap is required because the shared path is no longer operator-only.
item_9b_erase_source.bindings: Python: free #[pyfunction] taking `engine`, matching the purge/transition pattern, registered via wrap_pyfunction!; errors bridge through call_engine -> engine_error_to_py. TypeScript: async #[napi] method on the engine class + a public wrapper wrapping this.#native.eraseSource in intercept(). Both are thin pass-throughs with no client-side logic.
item_9b_erase_source.test: sdk_only_consumer_erases_without_cli (the design's named test) in src/rust/crates/fathomdb/tests/sdk_only_erasure.rs, file-gated `#![cfg(not(feature = "operator"))]` — i.e. it asserts the property in the exact build where an SDK-only consumer lives. Run it with `cargo test -p fathomdb --test sdk_only_erasure` (a feature-unified --workspace run turns `operator` ON and compiles this file to nothing, same as no_recovery_surface.rs).

item_11_doctor_orphan_provenance.engine: Engine::orphan_provenance() -> OrphanProvenanceReport, operator-gated, SELECT-only, opens no transaction. One `UNION ALL` over canonical_nodes + canonical_edges GROUP BY source_id, so a source spanning both tables reports as ONE bucket; the governed subtotal is SUMmed in the same query rather than counted separately so the two figures cannot drift.
item_11_doctor_orphan_provenance.semantics_chosen: `unerasable_rows` = rows with NEITHER source_id NOR logical_id. Rationale: purge keys on logical_id and erase_source keys on source_id, so such a row is reachable by no erasure verb and can never be deleted on request. This is the invariant 5c + migration step 21 established, and the verb is the operator-facing proof that it holds on a REAL database rather than only a fresh one.
item_11_doctor_orphan_provenance.exit_code: DOCTOR_FOUND_ISSUES (65) iff unerasable_rows > 0; OK (0) otherwise. `_legacy:` rows are REPORTED but are explicitly NOT an issue — they are fully erasable through the CLI recovery seam, merely not attributable.
item_11_doctor_orphan_provenance.posture: CLI-only, no SDK parity (same as dump-mutations / the dump-* family), so it adds nothing to the governed SDK surface.
item_11_doctor_orphan_provenance.tests: doctor_lists_per_source_counts (the design's named test), orphan_provenance_is_read_only (two consecutive runs must report an identical census — a verb that mutated would drift), orphan_provenance_help_exits_zero_with_usage. Also added to the shared DOCTOR_VERBS table (operator_cli.rs) and the parser verb table so AC-036/037/040a/040b now cover it — those suites re-ran 25 and 14 passed.

item_12_docs.erasure_boundary_negative_half: Explicitly documents what erase_source does NOT guarantee: copies you exported/replicated; SQLite FREE PAGES in the main db file absent a VACUUM; copy-on-write / wear-levelled / snapshotted filesystems; BACKUPS predating the erasure; the audit record itself; and the reserved namespace.
item_12_docs.non_pii_rationale_CORRECTED: The rule stands; its stated basis is replaced. v4 §3.6's premise ('the audit row retains source_id permanently, by design') is recorded as VERIFIED FALSE (defect D-A) and v4 §3.6 is called out as SUPERSEDED IN PART rather than quietly rewritten. The doc gives the correct basis — an erasure-audit row may not have been swept YET — and notes that 5b's retention exemption has since made the stronger premise actually true.
item_12_docs.defect_D_C_included: derive_logical_id = SHA256(lowercase(kind) + ':' + lowercase(name)); the case-folding is load-bearing for identity collapse but SHRINKS the preimage space, strengthening the dictionary-attack caution.
item_12_docs.changelog: A real `### Changed — BREAKING` section covering all three user-visible breaks: (1) mandatory source_id / SourceId newtype, with the deliberate no-shim rationale and a migration recipe; (2) 5a's projections_invalidated / rows_invalidated count INCREASE (v2 deletions now counted) — framed as the data-leak defect it was, not a cosmetic change; (3) 5b's retention `cap` now bounding SWEEPABLE rows.
item_12_docs.also_updated: docs/reference/cli.md (orphan-provenance verb, exit codes, --json example), docs/reference/python-api.md + typescript-api.md (erase_source / eraseSource), mkdocs.yml (Operations promoted to a section), docs/operations/index.md, dev/DOC-INDEX.md (5 rows).

non_regression.excise_source: 6 passed — the operator seam is unchanged by the split
non_regression.fathomdb_schema_migrations: 42 passed across the crate's targets, exit 0

INCIDENT_self_reported.what_happened: While probing whether the pyright errors were pre-existing, I ran `git stash push -u` followed by `git stash drop`. The `-u` swept the three UNTRACKED prior-sub-slice artifacts dev/plans/runs/0.8.20-slice-5{a,b,c}-output.json into the stash, and the drop deleted them from the worktree.
INCIDENT_self_reported.detected: immediately, by running `git status --porcelain` and noticing the three untracked entries had vanished
INCIDENT_self_reported.recovered: YES — fully. The dropped stash commit (1761b4c5a4ea703f1d9f05d0d22a7810d6d955af) was still reachable in the object store; its third parent (380febca8f680a5aa90b94fed667412a51f38907) is the untracked-files commit. All three files were restored from their blobs and verified BYTE-IDENTICAL via `git hash-object` against the stash blob shas (3341e942…, 51b89fcb…, 4ba3030c…) and re-parsed as valid JSON.
INCIDENT_self_reported.current_state: all three files present and untracked, exactly as they were at baseline; no other file was affected (my own work was already committed)
INCIDENT_self_reported.lesson: This is a variant of the trap the brief warned about ('5a destroyed its own uncommitted implementation with git checkout --'). My own work survived only because it was already committed. `git stash -u` is destructive to a sibling's untracked artifacts; a scoped `git stash push -- <paths>` or a throwaway clone would have been correct.
INCIDENT_self_reported.reported_because: the orchestrator should know these three files were briefly destroyed and restored, rather than discovering an unexplained mtime change

blockers_and_follow_ups.title: The Python half of the FU-5c-1 sweep + test_erase_source.py have never been EXECUTED
blockers_and_follow_ups.detail: Static evidence is strong (py_compile 0, ruff 0, AST residual audit clean, pyright shows zero new errors AND now resolves engine.erase_source), but no pytest process has run these files. A main-tree R-20-X1 pytest run is required before this can be called green. I decline to claim a green I did not observe.
blockers_and_follow_ups.recommendation: run pytest for src/python/tests in the main tree (or any environment where the native extension can be built) before the release gate

blockers_and_follow_ups.title: Asymmetric validation between erase_source and excise_source
blockers_and_follow_ups.detail: See implementation.item_9b_erase_source.JUDGEMENT_CALL_FOR_REVIEW. It is deliberate and forced by R-20-E8 + the 5c pin, but it is the one place a reviewer should confirm intent rather than assume it.

blockers_and_follow_ups.title: sdk_only_erasure.rs does not run under a feature-unified --workspace test
blockers_and_follow_ups.detail: The file is `#![cfg(not(feature = "operator"))]` because that is the only build where the SDK-only property is meaningful. `cargo test --workspace` turns `operator` ON via fathomdb-cli and compiles it to nothing. It must be invoked as `cargo test -p fathomdb --test sdk_only_erasure`. This mirrors the existing no_recovery_surface.rs situation, but CI should carry the explicit invocation or the test is silently inert.

blockers_and_follow_ups.title: scripts/agent-typecheck.sh exits 1 on 8 pre-existing pyright errors
blockers_and_follow_ups.detail: Error-set-diffed against baseline: zero new. Not fixed here (out of scope), but flagged because the script's non-zero exit could otherwise be misread as this slice's fault.
